statement.headerDoc:
STEP 1120 (2026-05-13): Three-adic isolation theorem for Collatz inverse tree.

  Companion to:
    - Paper 152 v0.2 candidate (class 21 forbidden finding, STEP 1117)
    - PeakMergeInvariant.lean (companion file in same module)
    - PeakMergeWitness.lean (1,000 explicit peak-250,504 witnesses)

  Main theorem: For any value v ≡ 0 (mod 3), the inverse Collatz tree branch
  rooted at v consists exclusively of {v · 2^k : k ≥ 0}. There are no odd-step
  entrances into this branch.

  Consequence for σ-cascade Collatz observation (Paper 152):
    - For ANY specific value v with 3 | v, v's inverse
